profile.strengths.heading: What I Can Bring You
profile.strengths.text: A young and versatile profile who can work independently. With strong learning abilities, I am capable of adapting to many situations.
profile.improvements.heading: My areas for improvement
profile.improvements.text: I often lack confidence in myself, double-check information I already know, or question work I'm capable of doing. So, if you're looking for someone with a keen sense of aesthetics, that's definitely not me.

interests.intro: Outside of computer science, here are the activities that rhythm my daily life:
interest.sports.title: Team Sports:
interest.sports.description: Former footballer turned basketball player, I now play and coach a youth team. I'm currently undergoing training to obtain my federal youth coaching certificate, an enriching experience that develops my teaching skills and team spirit.
interest.outdoors.title: Outdoor Activities:
interest.outdoors.description: Trekking and hiking enthusiast, I enjoy sharing these moments with friends and discovering new horizons.
interest.reading.title: Reading:
interest.reading.description: Passionate about science fiction (Asimov, Philip K. Dick) and mythology, whether classical or reimagined in contemporary works like those of Rick Riordan.
interests.closing: These passions cultivate my team spirit, perseverance, and intellectual curiosity.

modal1.title: Illustration of the Common Cultural Reference Concept
modal1.description: As part of the R2.13 resource, students were asked to create a newspaper front page reporting facts about the IUT of Arles. We interpreted the objective of this work as designing a credible and engaging front page for a newspaper aimed at IUT students ("L'Écho de l'IUT"). This work is based on knowledge of the concept of common cultural reference, namely a set of information shared by multiple parties, in this case the IUT students. For the layout, we relied heavily on the template present in the document distributed during the course where the instructions for this assignment were explained.
modal2.title: Qualitative Algorithm Comparison
modal2.description: As part of a cross-disciplinary end-of-semester project, we were asked to implement several different sorting algorithms and compare them, mainly on the criterion of execution speed. I subsequently wrote the above synthesis in English presenting the results and the conclusions we could draw from them. This project allowed me to better understand different sorting algorithms, the notions of time and space complexity as well as statistical analysis of results.
modal3.title: Organizational Analysis
modal3.description: As part of the economics resource in the first semester of the Bachelor's degree, we were asked to conduct a complete analysis of an organization of our choice. From a personal perspective, I mainly contributed to the SWOT and PESTEL analyses of the company "LeBonCoin".
modal4.title: Route Calculator
modal4.description:
This project was carried out as part of a first-year Computer Science Bachelor's project. The objective was to use knowledge on graph theory, data management as well as interface development using Qt in a single application responding to a clear need (Calculate a route). This software was made in C++ and required a lot of maintenance and research to obtain a final result close to my initial design. Technically, I implemented Dijkstra's algorithm for shortest path calculation, managed complex data structures (graphs, adjacency matrices) and data in CSV and XML files.

Thus, I was able to develop my skills in advanced algorithms, object-oriented programming and unit testing.

You will find in the compressed download file the software as well as the accompanying documentation, including the README.
modal5.title: Establishing a Requirements Document
modal5.description: For this work, we had to put ourselves in the shoes of a development team to start an application project. We undertook all the steps of starting a project (wireframing, preliminary study, establishing a requirements document). I mainly contributed to the design of the requirements document as well as the identification of functional needs, while managing the project, notably via the Jira platform. This project allowed me to develop my skills in Requirements Analysis, technical modeling and IT project management and collaborative tools.
modal6.title: Recipe Library
modal6.description: As part of the last first-year project, we had to create a recipe manager using the Qt Creator tool. This C++ project allowed me to do everything from A to Z, from class design to the structure of the graphical application to the datasets to be used. Since my partner participated very little, I realized that when things don't move forward fast enough, you have to take charge and manage on your own. Technically, I worked on a responsive graphical interface (Qt Designer), a filtering and search system and a Master/Detail Architecture. I was able to develop my skills in UML Design and Modeling, project management and integration testing. Below you will find the project README as well as a zip file containing the project in question, with its documentation and datasets.
modal7.title: Creating a Dynamic Web Application
modal7.description: To complete this project, it was necessary to find several web APIs to call so that one would be dependent on the results of the other. This work mainly focused on the use of the Javascript language and the Webpack configuration tool. This project allowed me to develop my skills in Web Development and Development Environment Configuration.
modal8.title: Keyp - Password Manager
modal8.description:
This Java project using JavaFX is a secure password manager developed in the second year of the Computer Science Bachelor's program. The application allows users to store and manage encrypted credentials with an expiration system and automatic alerts.

Technically, I implemented three interchangeable encryption algorithms (AES, DES, Blowfish) using the Strategy pattern, an MVC architecture with clear separation of responsibilities, and a persistence system with the DAO pattern. The JavaFX interface offers a dynamic table with color coding based on expiration urgency.

I was able to develop my skills in design patterns, applied cryptography (javax.crypto, SHA-256) and software architecture.

Below you will find the complete source code of the project along with its JavaDoc documentation.
